definition:
ἄγω, 
 [in LXX for בּוא hi., לקח, נהג, etc. ;] 
   1. to lead, bring, carry: with ace, before ἐπί, εἰς, ἕως, πρός and simple dative; metaphorically, to lead, guide, impel: Jhn.10:16 Rom.2:4, Heb.2:10, 2Ti.3:6, al. 
   2. to spend or keep a day: Luk.24:21, Act.19:38 
   3. Intrans., to go: subjunc., ἄγωμεν, Mat.2:46, al. (Cramer, 61; MM, VGT, see word).
 (AS)
